learning_outcomes.description: Understand theories, challenges and principles of risk communication
Identify the steps required to plan risk communication
Demonstrate good written risk communication and the principles that underpin it
Understand key issues related to different risk communication formats

assessments.task_description:
The aim of this assignment is to apply the knowledge, concepts and principles covered in Weeks 1 to 4 to analyse and understand a real-world example of risk communication. Through the case study analysis, you can reflect on what was done well and what could have been done better. 

You will choose a risk communication issue that focuses on environment and/or health. Your goal will be to conduct an analysis of the risk communication process in relation to that issue. The assignment has the following sections: Overview and brief timeline, description of the risk communication, reflection on the challenges, analysis of what was done well and what could have been improved.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via TurnItIn through the Course site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: This assessment gets you to develop written risk communication with the help of Generative AI (Gen AI). You will choose a risk communication scenario, develop a risk communication plan that outlines a purpose and objective, conduct an audience analysis and identify appropriate communication dissemination channels. With the help of Gen AI you will produce written risk communication content and be asked to critique it based on the principles and practices covered in the course. You will also be asked to establish how you checked the accuracy and credibility of any information gathered through Gen AI. The full assessment guidelines and marking rubric will be available in the Assessment 2 folder on the Course Blackboard site.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via TurnItIn through the Course site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The End of Semester Examination will be an in-person, paper-based exam for all students. The exam will be centrally administered and held during the Examination period. Students will be able to take a single two-sided page of handwritten or typed notes into the invigilated exam. A practice exam will be made available on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 1 - Introduction

The first seminar provides an introduction to the course and to risk communication theories.


Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 1 - Introduction The first seminar provides an introduction to the course and to risk communication theories.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 2 - Risk perceptions

In Week 2 we talk about and engage in activities that relate to theories and factors that influence people's risk perceptions.

Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 2 - Risk perceptions In Week 2 we talk about and engage in activities that relate to theories and factors that influence people's risk perceptions.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 3 - Risk communication challenges

Risk communicators face a range of challenges when it comes to their task. This week we discuss the different types of challenges that are faced when communicating about risk.

Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 3 - Risk communication challenges Risk communicators face a range of challenges when it comes to their task. This week we discuss the different types of challenges that are faced when communicating about risk.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 4 - Risk communication principles

In Week 4 you will be presented with core principles that guide effective risk communication.

Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 4 - Risk communication principles In Week 4 you will be presented with core principles that guide effective risk communication.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 5 - No formal class

There are no classes in Week 5 to provide time to complete the first assessment. Assessment consultation will be provided in the usual seminar timeslot.

Learning outcomes: L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 5 - No formal class There are no classes in Week 5 to provide time to complete the first assessment. Assessment consultation will be provided in the usual seminar timeslot.

learning_activities.topic:

This is the first of two seminars focused on planning the process of risk communication.

Learning outcomes: L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 6 - Planning risk communication (1) This is the first of two seminars focused on planning the process of risk communication.

learning_activities.topic:

Week 8 focuses on putting risk communication into practice and, in particular, developing written and visual risk communication.
